ideation: This sequence will show the robot inspecting a microbe, mimicking the action of looking closely at something small and potentially dangerous. The robot will use both arms to simulate holding a magnifying glass and carefully examining the microbe. This will involve a combination of precise movements and subtle changes in wrist and joint positions to create a believable and expressive animation sequence. The animation will start with the arms in a relaxed position and gradually move into a detailed inspection pose. The hands will mimic the act of holding a magnifying glass, and the subtle movements of the arms will show the robot's focus and concentration. This will use 5 frames to capture the progression of the action. Frame 1: The robot starts with both arms at its sides, neutral position. Frame 2: The robot brings both arms forward, slightly bent at the shoulder, palms facing each other as if it is holding an invisible magnifying glass. Frame 3: The robot brings the arms closer to its face as it focuses on the microbe. Frame 4: The arms move slightly to the side, changing the angle of observation. Frame 5: The robot returns to a neutral position, having completed its inspection.

frames.beat: arms hang at the sides, ready to examine
arms lift forward, palms ready to hold the lens
arms draw closer, focusing intently on the unseen
arms shift slightly, changing the viewing angle
arms return to sides, inspection complete
